content.claim: Academic explanations attribute the current SEN identification rise partly to greater awareness of developmental disorders and improved diagnostics leading to earlier, more accurate diagnoses, though this interpretive claim derives from qualitative interview research (21 teachers in one underdeveloped eastern region) and lacks population-level causal tests distinguishing awareness/diagnostic-practice effects from true prevalence change or incentive-driven over-identification.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Portuguese system reform lens
limitations: Based on small, geographically limited sample (21 interviews, one region)
Authors' interpretation rather than population-level causal decomposition
No empirical test distinguishing awareness/diagnostic-practice effects from prevalence or incentive-driven identification
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
